title: Enable strict_types where contracts matter
description: Declare strict_types and type hints so PHP rejects silent scalar coercion at call boundaries.
body: Use when a function's correctness depends on argument types (ids, money, flags). Do not rely on PHP's default coercive mode that turns '5' into 5 or true into 1 silently. Actions: add declare(strict_types=1) at the top of files you author (it governs calls made from that file), add scalar and return type hints, and use union/nullable types to express intent. Validation signals include a PHPUnit test asserting a TypeError is thrown when a wrong-typed argument is passed under strict mode. Risk boundary: silent coercion hides bugs that surface as wrong data far from the cause; never disable strict mode to make a wrong-typed call compile.
